stacks.title: Orchestrate the Work
stacks.tagline: One founder, a fleet of subagents.
stacks.why: Route the job through the orchestration front door: ensembles for hard calls, fan-out for parallel workloads, and independent skeptics before anything ships.

stacks.title: Founder Crew
stacks.why: Charter a small crew of specialist roles, pipeline their handoffs through staged artifacts, and keep the founder decisions with you.

stacks.title: Zero to One
stacks.tagline: Take a raw idea to product-market fit.
stacks.why: Diverge on ideas, validate the winner with real customer interviews, then pressure-test the economics before you build.

stacks.title: Karpathy-Style Discipline
stacks.tagline: Write less code, prove it works.
stacks.why: Surgical changes with verifiable success criteria, an over-engineering review that finds what to delete, then disciplined simplification and root-cause debugging.

stacks.title: Design Studio
stacks.tagline: Explore directions, build the winner, polish it.
stacks.why: Run a studio sprint to generate distinct concepts, construct the chosen direction with the design front door, judge it with explicit taste, and finish with a ship-ready polish pass.

stacks.title: Launch a Website
stacks.tagline: From positioning to a live URL.
stacks.why: Positioning and page-by-page copy, a fast modern toolchain, visual design that fits the brand, and a final polish pass before the domain goes live.

stacks.title: Ship a SaaS
stacks.why: Stand up the full production arc, keep it honest with test-first discipline, and land every change through a reviewed pull request.

stacks.title: Ship an iOS App
stacks.why: Architecture and HIG guidance, visual design through the design front door, tests that hold up under review, and polish that survives App Store scrutiny.

stacks.title: Hardware & D2C
stacks.why: Architect the device, app, and cloud together, take the design through CAD, PCB, and production gates, then sell it direct with a storefront.

stacks.title: Win the Client
stacks.why: Discovery-driven proposals with tiered pricing, a business case that survives scrutiny, and a site that closes the credibility gap.

stacks.title: Compound Engineering
stacks.why: Brainstorm and plan with the compound loop, review with its checklist, then compound what you learned back into reusable skills.

stacks.title: Superpowers Method
stacks.tagline: Plan it, delegate it, verify it.
stacks.why: The full obra/superpowers loop: write the plan, execute it with subagents, and refuse to call anything done before verification.
